title: Window Functions, Analytical Grain, and Time-Aware SQL · Result-Grain Case: Offset Functions
summary: Service leaders need rankings, running effort, previous-event comparisons, and rolling metrics without collapsing detail rows. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql offset functions increment in a time-aware service-level analytics report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use LAG, LEAD, FIRST_VALUE, and LAST_VALUE with deliberate ordering and frame behavior.
Choose ROW_NUMBER, RANK, DENSE_RANK, and NTILE with explicit tie semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Declare result or mutation grain, keys, NULL meaning, and transaction boundary; predict cardinality and invariants; run the smallest query; reconcile counts and plans; then test changed data and dialect limits.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Compare each ticket with its team's effort and severity peers, retain teams with no resolved work, and rank ties deliberately. Build named query stages, one changed window of 6 rows, and a reconciliation total. A defensible solution applies Offset Functions, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a time-aware service-level analytics report.

content.code:
SELECT team_id, ticket_id, severity,
       ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY team_id ORDER BY opened_at, ticket_id) AS arrival,
       LAG(opened_at) OVER (PARTITION BY team_id ORDER BY opened_at, ticket_id) AS previous_opened,
       COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY team_id) AS team_ticket_count
FROM tickets
ORDER BY team_id, arrival;
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
